title: N-Queens Problem
description: The N-Queens problem is the challenge of placing N queens on an N x N chessboard so that no two queens threaten each other. This means that no two queens can be in the same row, the same column, or on the same diagonal. Your task is to return all distinct solutions to the problem.

examples.explanation: For N = 4, there are two distinct solutions. In the first solution, queens are placed at (0, 1), (1, 3), (2, 0), and (3, 2). The second solution has queens at (0, 2), (1, 0), (2, 3), and (3, 1).
For N = 1, there is one solution where the queen is placed at the only position available (0, 0).
For N = 2, it is impossible to place two queens on a 2x2 board without them threatening each other, hence the output is an empty list.

constraints: The number of queens must be a positive integer.
hints: Consider using backtracking to explore potential placements of queens.
Keep track of which columns and diagonals are occupied to avoid conflicts.
You may want to use a recursive function to build possible solutions incrementally.
Think about how to represent the board and the positions of the queens effectively.
